business_environment.name: Understanding of Climate-Related and Environmental Risks

rubric.0: No evidence of assessment or understanding of climate-related and environmental risks in the business environment
rubric.1: High-level acknowledgment of climate risks without structured analysis of their impact on the business environment
rubric.2: Initial assessment of climate and environmental risks covering either transition or physical risks, but not both, and limited to short-term horizon
rubric.3: Documented assessment covering both transition and physical risks across short, medium and long term, with identified transmission channels
rubric.4: Comprehensive assessment integrated into strategic planning, covering multiple environmental risk drivers with scenario differentiation
rubric.5: Forward-looking, dynamic assessment with quantified impacts across time horizons, regularly updated and validated by external review

rubric.0: No monitoring of climate-related and environmental developments in the business environment
rubric.1: Ad hoc monitoring of major climate-related regulatory or market developments
rubric.2: Periodic monitoring process established but limited to regulatory developments only
rubric.3: Systematic monitoring of regulatory, market and technology developments with documented impact analysis
rubric.4: Comprehensive monitoring framework covering policy, technology, market sentiment and physical risk developments with regular board reporting
rubric.5: Real-time monitoring integrated into strategic decision-making with early warning indicators and peer benchmarking

rubric.0: No consideration of climate-related and environmental risks in business strategy
rubric.1: Climate risks mentioned in strategy documents but without concrete integration into business objectives
rubric.2: Climate considerations included in strategy with initial targets (e.g. green financing targets) but limited to one business line
rubric.3: Climate risks systematically considered in strategic planning with targets across multiple business lines and management body oversight
rubric.4: Climate strategy fully embedded in business strategy with quantified targets, transition plan and regular management body review
rubric.5: Comprehensive climate strategy with science-based targets, detailed transition plan, capital allocation aligned with Paris Agreement and public commitment

rubric.0: Climate-related and environmental risks not included in the risk appetite framework
rubric.1: Qualitative reference to climate risks in the risk appetite statement without specific limits or indicators
rubric.2: Initial climate-related risk appetite indicators defined but not yet operationalized with limits or thresholds
rubric.3: Climate-related risk appetite limits defined and operationalized for key risk categories (e.g. sector concentration limits for high-carbon sectors)
rubric.4: Comprehensive climate risk appetite with quantitative limits, escalation procedures and integration into ICAAP
rubric.5: Dynamic risk appetite framework with forward-looking climate metrics, scenario-dependent limits and alignment with transition plan targets

rubric.0: No assignment of climate-related risk responsibilities within the organisational structure
rubric.1: Climate risks mentioned as a topic but no dedicated roles or clear accountability assigned
rubric.2: Initial assignment of climate risk responsibilities to existing risk functions, but not yet embedded in three lines of defence
rubric.3: Climate risk responsibilities assigned across the three lines of defence with dedicated resources in risk management
rubric.4: Clear accountability structure with board-level oversight, dedicated C&E risk function and roles in all three lines of defence
rubric.5: Fully embedded governance with dedicated Chief Sustainability/Climate Risk Officer, board-level committee, regular skills assessment and external advisory

governance.name: Internal Reporting and Risk Data Aggregation

rubric.0: No internal reporting on climate-related and environmental risk exposures
rubric.1: Ad hoc internal reports on climate topics without structured data aggregation
rubric.2: Periodic internal reports produced but with limited risk data aggregation and incomplete portfolio coverage
rubric.3: Regular internal reporting with aggregated risk data on key climate risk exposures (e.g. sectoral concentrations, physical risk heatmaps)
rubric.4: Comprehensive internal reporting with granular risk data, dashboard views and regular management body reporting with actionable metrics
rubric.5: Integrated risk data infrastructure with automated aggregation, forward-looking risk indicators and real-time management body dashboards

rubric.0: Climate-related and environmental risks not incorporated into the risk management framework
rubric.1: Climate risks acknowledged as emerging risks but not mapped to existing prudential risk categories
rubric.2: Initial mapping of climate risks to credit, market, operational and liquidity risk categories with qualitative assessment
rubric.3: Climate risks integrated as risk drivers across all material prudential risk categories with documented risk identification and assessment processes
rubric.4: Comprehensive integration with quantitative risk assessment, dedicated risk indicators, and climate risk reflected in risk taxonomy and ICAAP/ILAAP
rubric.5: Dynamic risk management framework with climate risk embedded in all risk processes, validated by stress testing and with documented long-term horizon analysis

rubric.0: No consideration of climate-related risks in credit risk management
rubric.1: General awareness of climate factors in lending but no formal integration into credit processes
rubric.2: Climate risk factors considered in credit origination for selected high-carbon sectors only
rubric.3: Climate risk assessment integrated into credit origination, monitoring and review processes with sector-level risk classifications
rubric.4: Comprehensive credit risk integration with counterparty-level climate risk scoring, portfolio monitoring and concentration limits
rubric.5: Full integration with forward-looking credit risk models incorporating transition pathways, physical risk geolocation and client engagement strategies

rubric.0: No assessment of climate-related operational or reputational risks
rubric.1: Climate-related risks acknowledged in business continuity plans at a high level
rubric.2: Initial assessment of physical risk impact on operations (e.g. data centres, branches) and basic greenwashing risk awareness
rubric.3: Climate risks integrated into business continuity planning and operational risk framework, with reputational risk monitoring
rubric.4: Comprehensive operational risk assessment including physical risk to critical infrastructure, liability risk analysis and reputational risk early warning system
rubric.5: Fully integrated operational risk management with scenario-based business continuity testing, litigation risk monitoring and proactive reputational risk mitigation

rubric.0: No monitoring of climate-related factors in market risk positions
rubric.1: General awareness of climate-related market risk factors without systematic monitoring
rubric.2: Initial monitoring of climate-sensitive exposures in trading and investment books
rubric.3: Regular monitoring of climate-related market risk with stress-testing scenarios covering climate events
rubric.4: Comprehensive climate stress testing integrated into market risk framework with documented impact on VaR or equivalent measures
rubric.5: Dynamic market risk management with climate factor models, forward-looking scenario analysis and climate-adjusted investment policies

rubric.0: No assessment of climate-related impacts on liquidity risk
rubric.1: General acknowledgment of potential climate-related liquidity impacts without analysis
rubric.2: Initial qualitative assessment of climate risk transmission to liquidity through asset devaluation or funding channels
rubric.3: Climate-related liquidity risk factors identified and included in ILAAP with qualitative scenario analysis
rubric.4: Quantitative assessment of climate-related liquidity impacts integrated into liquidity stress testing and buffer calibration
rubric.5: Comprehensive climate-adjusted liquidity framework with dynamic stress scenarios, contingency funding plan integration and regular validation

rubric.0: No disclosure of climate-related and environmental risk information
rubric.1: Minimal qualitative statements on climate risks without structured disclosure policy
rubric.2: Initial climate disclosures present but not aligned with ECB expectations or Pillar 3 ESG requirements
rubric.3: Structured climate risk disclosures covering governance, strategy, risk management and metrics/targets, aligned with TCFD framework
rubric.4: Comprehensive disclosures with Pillar 3 ESG reporting, EU Taxonomy-aligned KPIs and detailed methodology explanations
rubric.5: Leading disclosures with full Pillar 3 ESG compliance, granular portfolio-level data, forward-looking metrics and external assurance

rubric.0: No key metrics or vulnerability disclosures on climate-related risks
rubric.1: Basic qualitative description of climate risk vulnerabilities without quantitative metrics
rubric.2: Initial quantitative metrics reported (e.g. financed emissions or EU Taxonomy GAR) but limited scope
rubric.3: Key metrics disclosed including financed emissions, sectoral exposure breakdowns and transition risk indicators
rubric.4: Comprehensive metrics with physical and transition risk exposure quantification, concentration analysis and Pillar 3 ESG templates completed
rubric.5: Full metrics suite with forward-looking indicators, climate VaR, alignment metrics, peer benchmarking and vulnerability heat maps
